Washington Water Power company
shall not pay but half the expense
of rebuilding the collapsed Howard
span-the bridge 1t warranted to
keep safe and sound--because they


made an agreement with the com.
pany, or the company made an
agreement with them, to that ef-
feet.


The city council passed a resolu.
ton ordering the board Of public
works to collect all the expense
from the Washington Water Power
company.


What influence has the company
used on Roy ard ONO so effectively
as to cause them to disobey the
orders of the city council?
And what's the mayor doing all
this time. He knows what's going


on. He's supreme in city political


affairs. He names all the men for


their jobs. names the commission


ers, the members of the water de-
partment, the health department.


and all o.her departments. He's on


the tnside to the negotiations be.
tween the commissioners and the
! Washington Water Power company,
yet he allows the company to han.
die his board of public works in


such way they seem to hold of.
fee mera'y as the company's board


of control on public salary.
Is the mayors administration
getting 10 be as rotten as some of


its successors?


1t has become question Of


whether the city council is running


the city or whether it Is the board


of public works. Is a resolution of


the city council worth anything?


1t don't seem to have been in this
| case.


But Joseph Phillips, councilman
from the Fifth ward, says:


"The board Of public works gets


its instructions from the legisla-


tive body of the city and the Pratt
resolution instructed the board to


collect all of the expense Of that
span from the company. II the


members Of the board don't adhere


to those instructions they should
| be thrown ont of their Jobs. A


two-thirds vote of the council can


do this without the mayors help.
The trouble is the members of the
board think themselves secure.


Backed by the mayor they think


they can do as they please. The


mayor got upon the floor of the


council chant ers twice already and


tried to tell the council what to do.
I'll tell the p'embers of the board


or the mayor to their faces that


thy're not right. The mayor has
not brains enough to run a wheel
barrow straight"


Captain Join Gray. councilman
from the Second ward, says:
THE Washington Water Power
company must pay all the expense
'of the fixing up of the bridge It


knocked down. The Pratt resolu-
ton hollis good. 1t looks to me
like this board is getting too friend.
ly with the company"
